title: Building a Crypto Store on GitHub Pages: Everything You Need to Get Started Without the Gatekeepers
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: From zero to live crypto store in one afternoon. GitHub Pages hosting, Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, NOWPayments integration.

content.text: You’re a creator in Nigeria, Pakistan, or Venezuela. Stripe, PayPal, and Gumroad are off-limits. Your bank won’t play nice with crypto exchanges. You’ve got digital products—ebooks, courses, art—but no way to sell them. This guide solves that. Using GitHub Pages, you’ll build a crypto-native store that bypasses gatekeepers. No subscriptions. No KYC. Just a one-time payment for lifetime access to your own infrastructure. By the end, you’ll have a fully automated store that accepts 300+ coins, delivers products instantly, and runs without a backend server. Let’s unblock your revenue.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

GitHub Pages is free, globally accessible, and doesn’t care where you’re from. It’s static hosting, so no server costs. The Core Shop Engine handles the backend—payment verification, automated delivery, PDF receipts—without needing a payment processor account. NOWPayments processes crypto payments without KYC, so buyers don’t need an account either. This stack is stateless, meaning it runs entirely on the client side. No databases. No servers. Just a static site that does everything a traditional store does, but without the middlemen. It’s the only way to sell digitally when every other platform has locked you out.
What You Need Before You Start

First, a GitHub account. Second, a crypto wallet to receive payments—use NOWPayments to generate a payment address. Third, the Full Stack Bundle ($79.99) for the Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard. Fourth, your digital products uploaded to a cloud storage service like IPFS or Google Drive. Finally, basic HTML/CSS knowledge to customize your storefront. If you’re a solo developer, you’ll appreciate the deployment guide included in the bundle. If you’re not, the Discord support will walk you through it. No coding required to launch, but customization is optional.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

Install the Core Shop Engine on your GitHub Pages repo. Configure NOWPayments with your wallet address and API key. The engine will verify payments on-chain and trigger instant delivery. For the frontend, integrate Web3 Wallet Connect to generate multi-chain QR codes and live price feeds. Test the flow on a mainnet like BSC or Polygon to minimize gas fees. Once a payment is confirmed, the engine sends the buyer a secure download link and a PDF receipt. The entire process is automated—you don’t lift a finger after setup. This is your payment layer, fully decentralized and unblockable.
The entire store stack — hosting, payments, delivery — has zero monthly costs after the one-time setup.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Deploy your repo to GitHub Pages. Share your live URL with your audience. The first sale is the proof of concept. Watch the Admin Dashboard for real-time transaction logs and order management. If a buyer pays with USDT on Tron, the dashboard will show it. If a download link expires, reissue it manually. Once you see the system working end-to-end, you’ll know it’s ready to scale. Your store is now a self-sustaining machine. No hosting bills. No platform fees. Just you and your buyers, peer-to-peer.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

Add more products by updating your cloud storage links in the Core Shop Engine. Use the Admin Dashboard to track analytics and optimize pricing. If you’re selling globally, enable multi-currency pricing via live feeds. For high-volume stores, replicate your repo across multiple GitHub accounts to avoid rate limits. The Full Stack Bundle includes priority updates, so you’ll get new features as we release them. This isn’t a temporary workaround—it’s a permanent solution. You’ve built infrastructure that no platform can take away.
How do I build a digital store that accepts crypto payments?

Yes, you can host a digital store on GitHub Pages for free. Combine it with the Core Shop Engine and NOWPayments, and you’ve got a fully functional crypto store without any hosting or transaction fees. GitHub Pages serves your static site, the Core Shop Engine handles backend logic, and NOWPayments processes crypto payments. The result is a decentralized store that runs on autopilot. No server costs. No platform cuts. Just a one-time payment for the tools, and you own the entire stack.
Test a real mainnet transaction before you promote — finding a bug with your own money costs less than finding it with a buyer's.
Related: How Creators Like You Are Getting Paid

If you’re ready to launch without piecing together fragments, grab the Full Stack Bundle ($79.99). It includes everything—Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, Admin Dashboard—plus priority updates and a deployment guide. See it in action at https://unchained-commerce.github.io/Shop/index.html. For a step-by-step NOWPayments integration tutorial, check our docs. This bundle is the fastest way to go from zero to selling, especially if you’re in a restricted country or just hate gatekeepers.
Your Action Checklist

Follow the setup sequence in order — skipping steps creates configuration errors that are hard to diagnose. First, create your NOWPayments account and add your wallet addresses and API key. Second, download and configure the Core Shop Engine with your NOWPayments credentials, product file paths, and delivery settings. Third, set up Web3 Wallet Connect with your product list and checkout configuration. Fourth, push everything to your GitHub repository and enable GitHub Pages on the repository. Fifth, configure your custom domain if you are using one — update DNS settings and confirm HTTPS is active. Sixth, complete a real mainnet test transaction and confirm the full pipeline: payment received, webhook fired, engine processed, receipt generated, delivery email sent, download link working. Seventh, check the Admin Dashboard entry for the test transaction. Only after step six and seven pass cleanly should you share your store URL publicly.
Four steps: deploy the engine, configure the wallet, test on mainnet, then and only then start promoting your store.
Full Stack Bundle — $79.99. Everything — Core Engine, Wallet Connect, Dashboard — plus priority updates, Discord support, and deployment guide. Your complete store infrastructure, one payment.
